observations.recommendation:
For the Local Chief Executive:
a. Direct the Inventory Committee to strictly adhere and comply to the provisions of COA Circular 2020-006 which provides for the Guidelines and Procedures in the Conduct of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE), Recognition of Items Found at Station, and Disposition for Non-Existing/Missing PPE Items, for the One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances of Government Agencies;
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
For the Municipal Accountant:
b. Exert more effort to come up with a record that will settle the differences with the property custodian/GSO records;
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
For the Municipal General Service Officer:
e. Conform to the proper format of the RPCPPE as provided under the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs and ensure that all details required in the RPCPPE are filled out to ensure proper reconciliation with the accounting records;
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
For the Municipal General Service Officer:
f. Expedite the disposal of the above-mentioned unserviceable properties to reflect the accurate and reliable amount of the PPE account;
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
For the Municipal General Service Officer:
g. Identify the accountable officers over the lost properties/not existing items and require the filing of the Request from Relief of Accountability; and
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
For the Municipal Assessor and Municipal General Service Officer
h. Expedite the titling of the remaining parcels of land in the name of the Municipality to safeguard the properties against third party claims.
observations.reason: Some parcels of land were not yet transferred in the name of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to validate the identified dormant bank accounts and recommend for its closure if the purpose of the fund has already been completed and satisfied or if it has no further use to avoid bank charges.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: a. Strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, and COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012, on the required analysis of dormant accounts by performing the regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of the existence of the receivables for strict monitoring and disposition of said accounts;
observations.reason: Request for write-off for dormant accounts was not yet made.

observations.recommendation: b. Enforce the immediate liquidation of fund transfers extended to implementing agencies from prior years, particularly for projects already implemented and refrain from processing fund transfers with unliquidated balances to avoid accumulation of unliquidated fund transfers; and
observations.reason: There were still unliquidated receivable accounts from implementing agencies.

observations.recommendation: c. Prepare the necessary documents as provided in Section 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 to support the request for write-off of the dormant accounts and submit to the Office of the Auditor for evaluation.
observations.reason: Request for write-off for dormant accounts was not yet made.

observations.recommendation: a. For the Local Chief Executive to require the offices/officials concerned to identify the gaps in the determination and implementation of development projects thereby come up with corrective measures to ensure optimal utilization of funds and timely delivery of value-added services to constituents;
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: b. For the Local Development Council and Local Finance Committee to assign specific timelines for the identified development projects to evaluate achievement rate and identify areas with a major shortfall or needs improvement to make timely and periodic remedial measures and determine the feasibility of implementation of prior year’s unimplemented projects and ensure the provision of AIP Reference Code for ease of tracking and budget linkage;
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
To Municipal Gen. Service Officer
c. Maintain stock cards for supplies in your custody to account for the receipt and disposition of the same;
observations.reason: Stock cards were still not maintained for supplies.

observations.recommendation: d. Prepare Monthly Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (MSSMI) duly supported by RISs Requisition and Issuance Slip (RIS) from end-users for submission to the Accounting Office for proper recording of issuances of inventories.

Conduct a physical inventory count of all the supplies and PPEs and prepare an inventory list following the prescribed format in COA Circular 2014-002. Reconcile the inventory records as reflected in the books with the actual physical count. Develop standard policy on the timely submission of Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS) and Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) of those already issued relief goods and supplies.
observations.reason: Inventories on hand still not reconciled with the Accounting records.

observations.recommendation: The BAC to prepare and submit the PMR to the GPPB. Aside from compliance with the rules and regulations in the submission of the PMR, the Local Chief Executive, through the BAC, should make use of this report as a tool in monitoring, assessing and improving the procurement process in the Municipality.

For the Municipal General Service Officer:
d. Conduct a complete inventory taking and submit the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) every January 31 of each year to the Office of the Auditor;
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: i. Compute for the depreciation charges of the identified PPEs and make the necessary adjustment on the affected charges;
observations.reason:
Implemented
Deficiencies on depreciation charges are still observed.

observations.recommendation: j. Locate documents pertinent to the LGU properties preparatory to the conduct of the mandatory physical inventory.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: a. Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office to prepare and submit the Local Supplemental Annual Investment Plan (AIP) in a prescribed format in compliance with DBM-Climate Change Commission (CCC)-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2014-1 dated August 7, 2014, as amended by DBM-CCC-DILG JMC No. 2015-1 dated July 23, 2015, and Budget Operations Manual for LGUs;

b. Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) or designated persons in charge of procurement activities to be updated and acquaint themselves with the latest GPPB issuances to ensure complete and strict compliance with the prescribed procedures and documentation. Also, strictly implement the required procedures and proper documentation as well as timing in the submission of vital documents such as Omnibus Sworn Statement (OSS) to evade the possible risks associated with procurement activities;

c. Direct the Municipal General Service Office to indicate the necessary data required the dates in the Purchase Request and Purchase Orders and date of receipt of the Supplier or Contractor to ensure the efficiency of procurement activities as well as the performance of delivery as the basis in the computation of the liquidated damages in case of delay in the delivery of procurement;
d. Direct the Municipal Accountant to check the completeness of the necessary documentary requirements for the procurement are complete, valid, and legal before payment is made; and
e. Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat to ensure full compliance with the reporting and posting requirements in the GPPB online portal and LGU website or full disclosure boards.

c. For the Local Chief Executive together with the LDC to identify the cause of non-implementation of the identified PPAs, evaluate its relevance to the current situation and its alignment with national government’s initiative, and henceforth undertake critical, urgent, and appropriate disaster response aid and measures to curtail and eliminate the threat of COVID-19.

a. The Local Chief Executive to direct the Local DRRM Officer and Council to ensure the preparation and submission of more complete and comprehensive Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan and ensure optimal utilization most especially the remaining idle funds. Additionally, evaluate the feasible implementation of the continuing appropriation PPAs to the current condition of the locality and proposed a more suitable projects that would help mitigate the disaster/pandemic that is prevailing in the local jurisdiction;
observations.reason: Unexpended STF is still not incorporated in the current year’s LDRRMFIP.

observations.recommendation: b. The Local DRRM Officer and Municipal Accountant to adhere and observed properly the accounting and reporting guidelines, viz: (a) regularly submit the monthly Report on Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund on or before the 15th day after the end of each month; (b) maintain the Subsidiary Ledgers for each type of Disaster Equipment; and (c) to draw an adjusting journal entry to recognize the asset which was erroneously charged and recorded to Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses and provide the equivalent depreciation expenses of the equipment.
observations.reason: Deficiencies in LDRRM are still observed in CY2021.

observations.recommendation: a. For the Municipal Accountant: Observe the use of Perpetual Inventory Method pursuant to Section 114 of NGAS Manual in recording the Inventory account;
observations.reason:
Implemented
The management has not been consistent in recording its inventories in accordance with the provision.

observations.recommendation: b. Record properly all purchases to its appropriate inventory account and maintain a supply ledger card for each type of inventory.
observations.reason:
Implemented
The management has not been consistent in recording its inventories in accordance with the provision.

observations.recommendation: a. For the Municipal Assessor to prepare and update the Assessment Roll and provide the same to the Municipal Treasurer as basis for collection of real property and special education tax;
observations.reason:
Implemented
The LGU is still waiting revision of the Province.

observations.recommendation: b. For the Municipal Treasurer to update the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR)/ Taxpayer’s index and furnish Accounting Office of the duly certified list of taxpayers, their amount due and collectible for the year. Henceforth, such list shall be provided at the beginning of each year;

c. For the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the RPT receivable under SEF to SET receivable and its corresponding deferred credit and record the beginning balance of the said receivables account and its related account based on the RPTAR/Tax Index Card report from the Office of the Municipal Treasurer.
a. Require the implementing offices or departments to re-visit the existing Memorandum of Agreement on trust funds in their possession and ensure that such MOAs are still effective;
observations.reason:
There still exist unrefunded/
unreturned funds.

observations.recommendation: b. Direct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Heads of implementing departments, to implement proper liquidation of the excess amount granted as well as the refund of excess project funds to the source agency, if so provided in the terms of the grant; and
observations.reason:
There still exist unrefunded/
unreturned funds.

observations.recommendation: c. Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to verify the existence of the funds in the bank, especially the excess or the long-outstanding balances, to facilitate the proper disposition of the funds.
observations.reason: Existence of funds were not verified and some long outstanding accounts remained undisposed.

observations.recommendation: Direct the implementing departments to follow the schedule of implementation of DRRM programs, projects, and activities as embodied in the approved LDRRMFIP. Consider adding the manpower complement as necessary.
observations.reason: DRRM was still not optimally utilized in CY2021 as reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
For BAC to:
a. Require each department/office to prepare complete PPMP for all PPAs for their respective departments/offices requirements;

For BAC to:
b. Consolidate all Project Procurement Management Plans (PPMPs) into the GPPB’s Annual Procurement Plan (APP) prescribed format;

We recommended and Management agreed on the following actions to
improve the financial reporting of the PPE account:
For the Local Chief Executive:
1. Direct the Inventory Committee to strictly adhere and comply to the
provisions of COA Circular 2020-006 which provides for the Guidelines and
Procedures in the Conduct of Physical Count of Property, Plant and
Equipment (PPE), Recognition of PPE Items Found at Station, and Disposition
for Non-existing/Missing PPE Items, for the One-Time Cleansing of PPE
Account Balances of Government Agencies;
For the Municipal Accountant:
2. Exert more effort to come up with a record that will settle the differences with
the property custodian/GSO records;
3. Effect the necessary adjustments on the erroneous recording of the advances
to contractors account to correct the reported balances of affected PPE
accounts. Henceforth, observe proper recording and classification of accounts
and transactions to avoid errors;
For the Municipal General Service Officer:
4. Conduct a complete inventory taking and submit the Report on Physical
Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) every January 31 of each
year to the Office of the Auditor;
5. Conform to the proper format of the RPCPPE as provided under the
Government Accounting Manual for LGUs and ensure that all details
required in the RPCPPE are filled out to ensure proper reconciliation with the
accounting records;
6. Expedite the disposal of the above-mentioned unserviceable properties to
reflect the accurate and reliable amount of the PPE account;
7. Identify the accountable officers over the lost properties/not existing items and
require the filing of the Request from Relief of Accountability; and
For the Municipal Assessor and Municipal General Service Officer
8. Expedite the titling of the remaining parcels of land in the name of the
Municipality to safeguard the properties against third party claims.

We recommended and Management agreed for the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to validate the identified dormant bank accounts and
recommend for its closure if the purpose of the fund has already been completed and
satisfied or if it has no further use to avoid bank charges.

Hence, we recommended and Management agreed on the following courses of
action:
1. Strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated
December 19, 2016, and COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012,
on the required analysis of dormant accounts by performing the regular and
periodic verification, analysis and validation of the existence of the receivables
for strict monitoring and disposition of said accounts;
2. Enforce the immediate liquidation of fund transfers extended to implementing
agencies from prior years, particularly for projects already implemented and
refrain from processing fund transfers with unliquidated balances to avoid
accumulation of unliquidated fund transfers; and
3. Prepare the necessary documents as provided in Section 8.3 of COA Circular
No. 2016-005 to support the request for write-off of the dormant accounts and
submit to the Office of the Auditor for evaluation.

In view of the above-mentioned observations, we recommended and
Management agreed on the following:
1. For the Local Chief Executive to require the offices/officials concerned to
identify the gaps in the determination and implementation of development
projects thereby come up with corrective measures to ensure optimal
utilization of funds and timely delivery of value-added services and benefits to
constituents;
2. For the Local Development Council and Local Finance Committee to assign
specific timelines for the identified development projects to evaluate
achievement rate and identify areas with a major shortfall or needs
improvement to make timely and periodic remedial measures and determine
the feasibility of implementation of prior year’s unimplemented projects and
ensure the provision of AIP Reference Code for ease of tracking and budget
linkage; and
3. For the Local Chief Executive together with the LDC to identify the cause of
non-implementation of the identified PPAs, evaluate its relevance to the
current situation and its alignment with national government’s initiative, and
henceforth undertake critical, urgent, and appropriate disaster response aid
and measures to curtail and eliminate the threat of COVID-19.

We recommended and Management agreed: 1) for the Local Chief Executive to
direct the Local DRRM Officer and Council to ensure the preparation and
submission of a more complete and comprehensive Local Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Investment Plan and ensure optimal utilization most especially the
remaining idle funds. Additionally, evaluate the feasible implementation of the
continuing appropriation PPAs to the current condition of the locality and proposed
more suitable projects that would help mitigate the disaster/pandemic that is
prevailing in the local government’s area of jurisdiction; and 2) for the Local DRRM
Officer and Municipal Accountant to adhere and observed properly the accounting
and reporting guidelines, viz: (a) regularly submit the monthly Report on Utilization
of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund on or before the 15th day after the
end of each month; (b) maintain the Subsidiary Ledgers for each type of Disaster
Equipment; and (c) to draw an adjusting journal entry to recognize the asset which
was erroneously charged and recorded to Other Maintenance and Operating
Expenses.

To Municipal Accountant;
a. To further investigate/scrutinize the remaining unsubstantiated balances and effect the necessary adjusting journal entries on the noted differences and stale checks;
To Municipal Treasurer:
b. To regularly monitor the released and unreleased checks and send written notice to the concerned payee regarding the existence of the checks before it becomes staled.
c. To return the original DVs and supporting documents of the staled checks to the Municipal Accountant who will prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the transaction as Accounts Payable.
To Municipal Accountant and Treasurer:
d. To validate the identified dormant bank accounts and suggest for its closure if the said purpose has already been satisfied or if it has no further use to avoid bank charges.

a. Direct the LDRRM Council to consider the timing of implementation of trainings to be included in the LDRRMFIP. Ensure that only trainings that can reasonably be accomplished during the year will be included; b. Direct the implementing departments to follow the schedule of implementation of programs, projects, and activities as embodied in the approved LDRRMFIP. Consider adding the manpower complement as necessary; c. Pursuant to Section 5.1.15 of COA Circular 2012-002, require the LDRRM Council to regularly review and update the said investment plan. Maximize the use of available resources by implementing subsequently identifying and funding new programs, projects, and activities that will address the needs of the LDRRMF Municipal Government as soon as the Local Finance Committee identified the unexpended and unrestricted balances.

a. Prepare LDRRMFIP inclusive of prior years’ unexpended balances. For prior years’ unexpended balances with uncertainty as to cash back up, coordinate with the Local Finance Committee (LFC) to identify unrestricted cash and savings to finance priority projects and cause immediate implementation thereof; b. Regularly review and update the said investment plan to consider current vulnerabilities and additions to fund caused by supplemental budgets.

a. Avoid lump sum appropriation, instead, ensure that the local budget operationalized the approved local development plan and contains identified objects of expenditures for each function, activity, and project;
Conduct regular monitoring and analysis of receivable accounts and request for write-off of dormant accounts.

Conduct a physical inventory count of all the supplies and PPEs and prepare an inventory list following the prescribed format in COA Circular 2014-002. Reconcile the inventory records as reflected in the books with the actual physical count. Develop standard policy on the timely submission of Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS) and Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) of those already issued relief goods and supplies.

a. Enforce the policy on waste segregation as mandated under RA 9003; and b. Prepare a 10-year Integrated Solid Waste Management Plan and submit to the National Solid Waste Management Commission for approval.

The BAC to prepare and submit the PMR to the GPPB. Aside from compliance with the rules and regulations in the submission of the PMR, the Local Chief Executive, through the BAC, should make use of this report as a tool in monitoring, assessing and improving the procurement process in the Municipality.

The Honorable Mayor should initiate the creation of Internal Audit Service (IAS) unit to strengthen internal control system of the Municipality in compliance to Section 1 of Administrative Order No. 70 dated April 14, 2003 and the provisions of RA No. 3456 as amended by RA No. 4177.

We recommended and management agreed on the following actions to
improve the financial reporting of the PPE account:
To Municipal Accountant:
1. Exert more effort to come up with a record that will settle the differences
with the property custodian/GSO records;
2. Compute for the depreciation charges of the identified PPEs and make the
necessary adjustment on the affected accounts.
To Municipal Gen. Service Officer:
3. Conduct a complete inventory taking and submit the Report on Physical
Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) every January 31 of
each year to the Office of the Auditor;
4. Expedite the disposal of the above-mentioned unserviceable properties
to reflect the accurate and reliable amount of the PPE account.

Hence, we recommended and management agreed on the following courses of
action:
To Municipal Accountant:
1. To further investigate/scrutinize the remaining unsubstantiated balances and
effect the necessary adjusting journal entries on the noted differences and
stale checks.
To Municipal Treasurer:
2. To regularly monitor the released and unreleased checks and send written
notice to the concerned payee regarding the existence of the checks before it
becomes staled;
3. To return the original DVs and supporting documents of the staled checks to
the Municipal Accountant who will prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV)
to record the transaction as Accounts Payable.
To Municipal Accountant and Treasurer:
4. To validate the identified dormant bank accounts and suggest for its closure
if the said purpose has already been satisfied or if it has no further use to
avoid bank charges.

We recommended and management agreed on the following actions to
improve the financial reporting of the Inventory account:
To Municipal Accountant:
1. Observe the use of Perpetual Inventory Method pursuant to Section 114 of
NGAS Manual in recording the Inventory account;
2. Record properly all purchases to its appropriate inventory account and
maintain a supply ledger card for each type of inventory.
To Municipal Gen. Service Officer:
3. Maintain stock cards for supplies in your custody to account for the receipt
and disposition of the same.
4. Prepare Monthly Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (MSSMI) duly
supported by Requisition and Issuance Slip (RIS) from end-users for
submission to the Accounting Office for proper recording of issuances of
inventories.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended and management agreed on the
following courses of actions:
1. For the Municipal Assessor to prepare and update the Assessment Roll and
provide the same to the Municipal Treasurer as basis for collection of real
property and special education tax;
2. For the Municipal Treasurer to update the Real Property Tax Account
Register (RPTAR)/ Taxpayer’s index and furnish Accounting Office of the
38
duly certified list of taxpayers, their amount due and collectible for the year.
Henceforth, such list shall be provided at the beginning of each year;
3. For the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the RPT receivable under SEF to
SET receivable and its corresponding deferred credit and record the
beginning balance of the said receivables account and its related account
based on the RPTAR/Tax Index Card report from the Office of the
Municipal Treasurer.

In view of the above-mentioned observations we recommended and
management agreed on the following:
1. A cash analysis should be made and the Local School Board should take into
consideration the total available resources in the preparation of SEF annual
budget. SEF unrestricted funds as identified at year end should be
appropriated in the next year’s budget based on the guidelines in the
utilization of SEF;
2. Observe timely preparation, review and approval of the SEF budget.
Consider in the budget preparation the inclusion of programs, projects and
activities in the Annual Investment Program to effectively prioritize
education programs which are envisioned by the Municipality as well as
complementing the same to the education budget of the national government;
and
3. Abide with the mandate of Section 100 of RA No. 7160 to conduct regular
meeting or as often as may be necessary so that relevant issues may be
discussed and changes in annual budget through supplemental budgets may
be done.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended and management
agreed that the Local Chief Executive will:
1. Require the implementing offices or departments to re-visit the existing
Memorandum of Agreement on trust funds in their possession and ensure
that such MOAs are still effective;
2. Direct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Heads of
implementing departments, to implement proper liquidation of the amount
granted as well as the refund of excess project funds to the source agency, if
so provided in the terms of the grant; and
3. Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to verify the
existence of the funds in the bank, specially the excess or the long-
outstanding balances, to facilitate the proper disposition of the funds.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended for the:
1. Local Chief Executive and the Sangguniang Bayan to stop granting bonuses
and incentives that are devoid of legal basis and refrain from approving
disbursements that are tainted with irregularities;
2. Municipal Accountant to ensure that disbursements against public funds are
supported with complete relevant documents before processing any
disbursement.

Given the foregoing observations, we recommended and management agreed
that the Local Chief Executive will:
1. Direct the LDRRM Council to consider the timing of implementation of
trainings to be included in the LDRRMFIP. Ensure that only trainings that
can reasonably be accomplished during the year will be included;
2. Direct the implementing departments to follow the schedule of
implementation of programs, projects, and activities as embodied in the
approved LDRRMFIP. Consider adding the manpower complement as
necessary;
3. Pursuant to Section 5.1.15 of COA Circular 2012-002, require the LDRRM
Council to regularly review and update the said investment plan. Maximize
the use of available resources by subsequently identifying and funding new
programs, projects, and activities that will address the needs of the
Municipal Government as soon as the Local Finance Committee identified
the unexpended and unrestricted balances.
Comply strictly the submission of these disbursement vouchers and supporting documents in accordance with existing regulations.
observations.managementAction: The management explained that they already exerted effort in locating the disbursement vouchers but they were still not able to locate those.
observations.reason:
Notice of Disallowance already issued.
AAR 2017

observations.recommendation: Conduct regular monitoring and analysis of receivable accounts and request for write-off of dormant accounts.

The Special Disbursing Officer, in the person of Local Chief Executive submit to ICFAU the liquidation of 1st quarter additional confidential fund amounting to P225,275.00. Otherwise, reconstructs the balance of outstanding Advances to Special Disbursing Officer Account and reinstate the previously recorded liquidation not duly supported by necessary documents.
The Municipal Accounting Office to establish and maintain a special account for the transactions under the 20% Local Development Fund.

The BAC to prepare and submit the PMR to the GPPB. Aside from compliance with the rules and regulations in the submission of the PMR, the Local Chief Executive, through the BAC, should make use of this report as a tool in monitoring, assessing and improving the procurement process in the Municipality.

The Honorable Mayor should initiate the creation of Internal Audit Service (IAS) unit to strengthen internal control system of the Municipality in compliance to Section 1 of Administrative Order No. 70 dated April 14, 2003 and the provisions of RA No. 3456 as amended by RA No. 4177.

We recommended and reiterated and Management agreed on the following:
a. Direct the Inventory Committee to strategize the conduct of complete
physical inventory taking of all the assets and properties of the LGU and
prepare updated RPCPPE using the prescribed form as presented in Annex
C;
b. Direct the MAO to maintain property ledger cards to facilitate the
reconciliation and recognition of depreciation expenses;
c. Direct the Inventory Committee to ensure that the reconciliation process
between the physical inventory records of the MGSO and accounting
records are properly in placed, to come up with a reliable report.

In view of the above-mentioned observations we recommended and Management agreed
to the following:
The Acting Municipal Accountant to:
a. Exert efforts to trace previous transactions and to capture necessary
information pertaining to the physical and financial transactions relative to
the unrecorded and closed depository accounts. If found valid, prepare
entry to recognize cash balances from unrecorded depository account and
exclude from the account the balances from closed depository accounts;
b. Identify and validate the unsubstantiated discrepancies and prepare
necessary journal entries to present the correct balances of cash in banks
accounts. Likewise, take up necessary adjustment in its book of accounts
for stale checks;
c. Regularly prepare the bank reconciliation statement of all depository
accounts and furnish the Municipal Treasurer and COA Auditor a copy with
bank statements, JEV recording reconciling items as well as a copy of the
letter to the respective bank informing them of errors noted in the BRS
(other than those resulting from the timing of recording of transactions);
The Acting Municipal Treasurer to:
d. Prepare schedule of unreleased checks monthly and forward the same to
accounting and send written notices to the concerned payees before
checks become staled;
The Acting Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to:
e. Regularly monitor the cash balances and transactions to determine proper
use of the accounts to avoid bank fees for insufficient funds; and
f. Close depository accounts in which the purpose had already been satisfied
to avoid possible dormancy charges or below maintaining balance bank
charges.

In view of the above-mentioned observations we recommended and Management agreed
on the following:
a. The Accountant to stop granting additional cash advances to officials and
employees until the previous ones have been liquidated and/or settled;
b. Continue the issuance of demand letters to the personnel with unliquidated
cash advances to facilitate the immediate liquidation;
c. Strictly deny clearance from money and property accountability to resigning,
retiring and transferring officials and employees with outstanding cash
advances; and
d. The Accountant and Treasurer to withhold the salary of officials and
employees who fail or refuse to liquidate and/or settle their cash advances
after giving due notice and final demand.

In view of the above-mentioned observations we recommended and Management agreed
on the following:
For BAC to:
a. Require each department/office to prepare complete PPMP for all PPAs for
their respective departments/offices requirements;
b. Consolidate all Project Procurement Management Plans (PPMPs) into the
GPPB’s Annual Procurement Plan (APP) prescribed format;
c. Ensure compliance with RA 9184 by adopting competitive bidding and
resorting to alternative methods of procurement only in highly exceptional
case.
For BAC and Accounting to:
d. Furnish the audit team a copy of perfected contract and each of all the
documents forming part thereof by reference or incorporation for technical
evaluation;
e. Ensure all government transactions are duly supported with complete
documentary requirements under COA Circular 2012-001 and other
existing issuances of COA.

We recommended and reiterated and Management agreed that the Acting Municipal Accountant in
coordination with the Heads of implementing units, to communicate, implement proper
liquidation and make the immediate reconciliation of the fund transfers with source agencies
to support the balance reflected in the Financial Statement. Also, return the excess or
unutilized balances to source agencies concerned.
We recommended and Management agreed to expedite the conduct of study
and institutionalization of the TechVoc Center. The Municipal Development Council (MDC), Local
Finance Committee (LFC) and Departments, lead by the LCE, should closely coordinate in
developing a concrete plan on the institutionalization and operationalization of the
TechVoc Center bearing in mind that project implementation does not stop on the
construction of the structure but should continue up to the sustainable delivery of services that
directly contributes to the achievement of goals and objectives of the LGU.

We recommended and Management agreed for the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the
MDRRM Council together with the MDRRM Officer to:
a. Prepare LDRRMFIP inclusive of prior years unexpended balances. For prior
years unexpended balances with uncertainty as to cash back up,
coordinate with the Local Finance Committee (LFC) to identify unrestricted
cash and savings to finance priority projects and cause immediate
implementation thereof; and
b. Regularly review and update the said investment plan to consider current
vulnerabilities and additions to fund caused by supplemental budgets.

We recommended and Management agreed on the following:
a. As a reiteration, avoid lump sum appropriation, instead, ensure that the
local budget operationalized the approved local development plan and
contains identified objects of expenditures for each function, activity, and
project;
b. The Local Finance Committee (LFC) to identify both the unrestricted cash
of the LGU and projects with no cash back up. Use the unrestricted cash
and savings from completed projects to finance priority projects so that
the development projects shall be implemented for the benefits of the
constituents;
c. Adopt austerity measures on the financial operations of the LGU to lessen
incurrence of big amount of cash overdraft/deficit. Also, intensify LGUs
collection including refund of the disallowances.

We recommended and Management agreed for the Local Chief Executive to
direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to maintain special account in the General
Fund for development projects funded from the 20% of the IRA. To facilitate the
setting up of the special account as of cut-off date chosen by the Management, the
Acting Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer
should cause first the identification of the continuing appropriations and liabilities
under 20% LDF and establish their balances with the equivalent cash account.
Henceforth, transactions involving the 20% LDF should be recorded under the special
account. Through this, the Management can start to maintain the special account for
20% LDF mandated by law and required by sound financial management.

1. Use the maximum monthly compensation of P5,000.00 per month as basis in computing the employer share as provided in RA 9679 or a maximum of P100 per month regardless of the amount that the employee contributes for the personal share. Also, make representations with HDMF to consider the over-remittance of HDMF Premiums as advance payment on the personal share of the LGU.
2. The Municipal Accountant to comply with the policy set by HDMF and remit contributions and loan amortization within the due date to avoid incurrence of penalty. Henceforth, future payment of penalty that may be imposed due to delayed remittance shall be paid/borne by the persons responsible.

The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to present fairly the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts as well as the reconciliation statements in accordance with PPSAS and COA Circular 2016-004.
The LCE to direct the implementing departments to execute without delay the projects which are determined to still address the present needs of the intended beneficiaries.

1. Enforce the policy on waste segregation as mandated under RA 9003; and
2. Prepare a 10-year Integrated Solid Waste Management Plan and submit to the National Solid Waste Management Commission for approval.

The BAC Secretariat to comply with the requirements under RA 9184 by posting the invitation or request for submission of price quotation, as well as the other required documents, in the PhilGEPS website and the Procuring Entity's website and premises.

The BAC Secretariat submit the original contracts and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor strictly within five (5) days from execution, as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001 in order that management may avail of the timely and relevant technical and auditorial evaluation results and/or corrective measures. Also, the status of various projects and submit schedules and/or list of projects to ensure the validity of the CIP account balances appearing in the year-end financial statements.

Municipal officials concerned to ensure that all the requirements provided under COA Circular No. 2007-001 are strictly complied with prior to the release of financial assistance to NGOs/POs including the proper accounting thereof. Also, demand from the NGOs/POs the liquidation of the funds. Monitoring and evaluation of the project shall likewise be done.
We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Accounting Office establish and maintain a special account for the transactions under the 20% Local Development Fund.

We recommended that the BAC prepare and submit the PMR to the GPPB. Aside from compliance with the rules and regulations in the submission of the PMR, the Local Chief Executive, through the BAC, should make use of this report as a tool in monitoring, assessing and improving the procurement process in the Municipality.

The Honorable Mayor should initiate the creation of Internal Audit Service (IAS) unit to strengthen internal control system of the Municipality in compliance to Section 1 of Administrative Order No. 70 dated April 14, 2003 and the provisions of RA No. 3456 as amended by RA No. 4177.
observations.managementAction: The   selection process for the members of IAS unit of the Municipality

observations.recommendation:
Reconcile / Confirm with the records of the different Source Agency and remit the unexpended amounts.
Complete the unfinished projects and return to source agencies the unexpended balances.
Communicate with source agencies on projects which were found not feasible and make representation to reprogram the amounts to other projects of the local government unit.

Enforce the policy on waste segregation as mandated under RA 9003; and
Prepare a 10-year Integrated Solid Waste Management Plan and submit to the National Solid Waste Management Commission for approval.

We recommended and management agreed that the LGU maximize the implementation of the various projects, programs and activities under the 20% Local Development Fund that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development.

We recommended and management agreed to maximize the utilization of available resources to achieve utmost disaster preparedness. Prepare LDRRMFIP that includes current and previous years’ unexpended LDRRMF to maximize the programs, projects and activities relating to mitigation, prevention and preparedness.

We recommended and management agreed that the BAC Secretariat comply with the requirements under RA 9184 by posting the invitation or request for submission of price quotation, as well as the other required documents, in the PhilGEPS website and the Procuring Entity's website and premises.

We recommended that all the planned programs, projects and activities incorporated in the LGU’s budget should be included in the Annual Investment Program. Revisit the guidelines set on the harmonization of local planning, investment programming, revenue administration, budgeting and expenditures management in the DBM-NEDA-DILG-DOF Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1, series of 2007 dated March 8, 2007

We recommended to maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund allocated for the implementation of various projects, programs and activities are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development and optimally utilize the 20% Development Fund to help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.
Stop implementing projects chargeable to the 20% LDF which do not partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures.

We recommended that all programs and activities that are to be implemented by the LGU should contain specifics as to the amount, name, nature and place to be undertaken, Henceforth, lump sum appropriation should be avoided. Instead, ensure that the local budget operationalizes the approved local development plans and contains identified objects of expenditures for each function, activity and projects.
We recommended that the management follow the standard process of procurement and avoid the practice of purchases thru reimbursement.
We recommended that the Management withhold the salary of officials and employees who fail or refuse to liquidate and/or settle their cash advances after giving due notice and final demand.
We have recommended that the concerned LGU officials prepare effective and sustainable plans for the implementation of the projects under the 20% Local Development Fund.

We recommended that the BAC Secretariat submit the original contracts and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor strictly within five (5) days from execution, as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001 in order that management may avail of the timely and relevant technical and auditorial evaluation results and/or corrective measures.
Also, review the status of various projects and submit schedules and/or list of projects to ensure the validity of the CIP account balances appearing in the year-end financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal officials concerned ensure that all the requirements provided under COA Circular No. 2007-001 are strictly complied with prior to the release of financial assistance to NGOs/POs including the proper accounting thereof. 
Also, demand from the NGOs/POs the liquidation of the funds. Monitoring and evaluation of the project shall likewise be done.

We strongly recommended that the Municipal Mayor assume as Special Disbursing Officer (SDO) or designate the PNP Provincial Director as SDO of cash advances from intelligence fund pursuant to DILG MC No. 99-100.
Also, restore to the proper accounts all cash advances from the intelligence fund which have no Credit Notice from COA.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the validity of the cash advances through confirmation from the concerned officials/employees and demand immediate liquidation thereof.
